notes: All 5 workers agree: Depth Guard leaf-lock is well-specified in agent files
All workers agree: enforcement is policy-based (text instructions) not capability-hardened (bash remains available)
Consensus gap: no external validation layer exists - shell scripts trust agent self-reporting
Consensus gap: lint script covers core invariants but has significant blind spots (SKILL.md not linted, model lists not validated)

key_findings: Worker leaf-lock: tools correctly exclude task/ask_user; text-based policy forbids spawning but bash tool remains a theoretical escape hatch
Commander depth budget: properly specifies max_depth=3, 50 squads x 5 workers = 250, with partial/failed fallback
Skill-level gap: no commander count cap in metaswarm mode; depth enforcement delegated entirely to commander self-governance
Lint coverage gap: tests/depth-guard-lint.sh does not lint SKILL.md, only partially checks worker prompt block (2/4 lines), and skips model pool validation
Failure mode gap: no external timeout/circuit-breaker for stuck commanders; no shell-level validation that reported status matches actual launch counts
recommendations: Add SKILL.md depth-guard checks to lint script
Add external launch-count validation (compare swarm-state.json telemetry vs reported status)
Consider adding a metaswarm commander count cap in the skill
Complete the worker prompt block lint (check all 4 lines, not just 2)
